---
title: "Activities"
format: html
---

---
title: "Activities"
---

## Lawrence University Investment Club

At Lawrence University I founded the Investment Club and have served as both Vice President and President. The work of the club centers on developing rigorous investment research and building stock proposals that can withstand serious scrutiny. Managing risk and guiding peers through the discipline of financial analysis has been among the most formative experiences of my undergraduate years. The club has pushed me to think carefully about evidence, to argue precisely, and to understand how broader economic forces shape individual decisions.

---

## Hockey

Hockey has been the defining thread of my life. Growing up in the Greater Boston area the sport became central to how I understand competition and preparation. Playing junior hockey allowed me to travel across the country competing at a high level in a sport I love. Those years on the road introduced me to players and communities from every corner of the nation and gave me an understanding of competition and perseverance that no classroom could replicate.

The experience of junior hockey was inseparable from the long hours spent in the car between rinks and cities. Those drives became their own kind of education. My mother made all of it possible. She spent countless hours in cold arenas and on long roads making sure I could pursue the game at the level I wanted to. Her commitment to my development as a player and as a person is something I carry with me. I now compete as a member of the Lawrence University hockey team. Hockey rewards composure under pressure and punishes carelessness and those lessons have shaped the way I approach academic work and professional challenges alike.

[View my Elite Prospects profile →](https://www.eliteprospects.com/player/504543/joseph-dee)

---

## Personal

Golf is one of my favorite ways to spend time away from work and study. I grew up playing with my father and those rounds remain some of the most enjoyable I can point to. The game is patient and unforgiving in equal measure. It asks you to compete honestly against yourself and to accept the results of your own decisions.

There is something that cannot quite be explained about the relationship between a boy and his dog. Ulysses is my chocolate Labrador and from the moment he entered my life he became something more than a pet. He is a presence. He has a way of meeting you exactly where you are without asking anything of you in return. The bond between a person and a dog is one of the oldest relationships in human history and I think it endures because it is uncomplicated by the expectations and conditions that define so many other relationships.
